mechanism_narrative: A persistent quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the federal funds rate and declines in industrial production signal a supply-constrained tightening cycle that raises discount rates without supporting earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the interaction term's coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes earnings revisions downward and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high INDPRO growth periods during late-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for S&P 500 earnings yield.

mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly housing starts indicate strengthening consumer confidence and credit availability, which mechanically supports forward equity returns over the next week as capital flows into cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant difference between top and bottom quartiles of HOUST growth, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC consistently over three consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5% year-over-year growth threshold, inflation expectations embed into longer-term discount rates, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and contraction in real consumer credit signals a credit-fueled labor boom that typically peaks before equity corrections, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/RSAFS signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of episodes, or the interaction effect disappears when adjusting for VIX term structure.

mechanism_narrative: During quantitative tightening phases, a monthly contraction in real consumer credit directly reduces household purchasing power, which mechanically depresses forward equity returns over the next week as earnings estimates are revised lower.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of RSAFS contraction months during QT phases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year walk-forward test.
